headline: CoreWeave and Nebius tumble as Meta threatens cloud AI market
detail: Meta positioning as a fresh competitor in cloud AI directly pressures the AI infrastructure buildout narrative that has driven semiconductor and chip names all year. This is sentiment-negative for your chip-heavy book - CRWD, SMH, SOXL all trade on the same AI tailwind story. Not a fundamental collapse but a real confidence dent on crowded AI trades.
rec: No trade triggered by this headline alone, but watch SMH for further weakness - if it breaks toward $580, your SOXL stop at $200 becomes much more likely to get tested. No new buys here.

headline: Nvidia betting on trillion-dollar robotics boom - hidden ways to trade it
detail: Robotics as Nvidia's next growth vector is a longer-term narrative, not a today catalyst. For your holdings it's background-positive for CRWD and SMH but doesn't change the near-term technical picture or the margin pressure you're already carrying.
rec: Interesting longer-term angle but no actionable entry today - scanner shows ARM (entry 336.80-337.13, stop 336.18) as a Grade B accumulate setup that ties into the Nvidia/robotics ecosystem, but the daily higher timeframe is in premium, meaning you'd be chasing. Pass for now.

headline: Jim Cramer says use Wednesday's market rotation to your advantage
detail: Rotation days mean money moves between sectors, not necessarily out of the market. For a tech-concentrated margin trader this cuts both ways - some of your names could get sold to fund other sectors even if the broader market holds up. It also means volatility spikes intraday.
rec: No specific trade, but on a rotation day with margin exposure, sitting on your hands is a valid move. Protect SOXL and SPAL stops before trying to pick rotation winners.

headline: U.S. auto industry faces uncertainty without USMCA extension
detail: Trade deal uncertainty adds to the macro noise around tariffs and supply chains, which indirectly pressures semiconductor names through the automotive chip demand channel (TSM, SMH have auto exposure). Not an immediate catalyst but adds to the risk-off undercurrent in chips.
rec: No action today, but TSM shows a scanner accumulate setup (entry 443.34-443.79, stop 442.47, Grade B) if you wanted chip exposure with better risk structure than your current leveraged names - only relevant if you first reduce SOXL/SPAL risk.
